key_insight: An active learning loop can dramatically reduce the data needed to discover sparse governing equations by repeatedly sampling the most informative regions for SINDy model identification.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient system identification for ODEs and PDEs when measurements are expensive, sparse, or limited by experimental access.
method: Use E-SINDy to estimate epistemic uncertainty over candidate dynamics.
Select new samples from regions predicted to be most informative, rather than sampling uniformly at random.
Iterate data collection and sparse regression until the governing equations are recovered.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on the Lorenz system across multiple noise levels and data budgets, plus Burgers' and Kuramoto-Sivashinsky PDEs, and it matched or outperformed random sampling with far fewer samples.
watch_item: Performance may depend on reliable uncertainty estimates and on whether the system contains clearly informative regions; highly noisy or poorly structured data could make active sampling less effective.
paper_title: How Low Can You Go? Active Learning for Sparse Model Discovery in the Ultra-Low-Data Limit
